title: Vision provider chain + 429 circuit breaker
current_state: MediaTools.analyze_image picks Gemini if GEMINI_API_KEY present, else Claude Vision. No fallback when Gemini returns 429. User-visible result: raw RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED stack trace fragment lands in Telegram reply. Same shape will happen for STT (Whisper free tier) and TTS providers over time — there is no general provider-chain abstraction.
structural_problem: Provider selection is hardcoded per-tool. Adding a fallback today means copy-pasting a try/except. Circuit-breaker state would have to be reimplemented per call site. We already solved this once for compression (T-092: Groq→Haiku→hard truncate); the pattern needs to be extracted.
target_state: agent/provider_router.py exports ProviderRouter(providers=[...], cooldown_parser=...). Each provider is a callable that raises on failure; router walks the list, parses 429 retryDelay if present, marks provider in cooldown, advances to next. 3 consecutive failures of a single provider open a 5-minute circuit. All failures and cooldown opens recorded via track_silent('vision.<provider>'). tools_media.analyze_image and analyze_video go through the router.
design_notes: ProviderRouter API: register(name, callable, cooldown_after_429: bool=True); call(*args, **kwargs) -> result | raises AllProvidersExhausted
Cooldown state: {provider_name: cooldown_until_iso}; in-memory, no persistence (process restart = clean slate)
429 parser: regex for 'retry in (\d+(?:\.\d+)?)s' on Google errors, 'retry-after' header on Anthropic 429s
Circuit breaker: per-provider failure counter; reset on any success; open at 3; cooldown 5 min
Vision chain order: gemini-2.0-flash → gemini-1.5-pro → claude-haiku vision → claude-sonnet vision (most expensive last)
success_criteria: Forced 429 from Gemini → next call routes to Claude Vision without user-visible error
3 consecutive Gemini 429s → 4th call skips Gemini entirely for 5 min
track_silent records each cooldown open/close event
MediaTools.analyze_image keeps its return contract ({success, analysis, backend, ...})
risk_notes: Cost risk: cooldown on cheap providers pushes traffic to expensive Claude Vision. Mitigation: warn-level log on any chain step ≥ index 2; daily digest surfaces if Claude Vision call count > 10/day.
migration_plan: 1. Write agent/provider_router.py with ProviderRouter class + AllProvidersExhausted exception
2. Write testing/test_provider_router.py — synthetic providers, force 429, assert order + cooldown
4. analyze_image / analyze_video build router on first call, cache it
test_plan: test_429_advances_to_next — provider 0 raises 429; provider 1 returns; result matches provider 1
test_all_fail_raises_exhausted — every provider raises; AllProvidersExhausted raised with chain history
test_circuit_opens_after_3_failures — fourth call skips the failing provider
test_circuit_closes_after_cooldown — patch time; assert provider re-tried
test_retry_delay_parsed_from_google_error — assert cooldown_until matches parsed retryDelay
